title: Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. This family is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. Members of the Rutaceae family are primarily native to warm, temperate, and tropical regions around the world. The genus Zanthoxylum is a diverse group with various species, many of which are used in traditional medicine. Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides is native to tropical Africa, where it is often found in savannas and grasslands.
uses: Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides, also known as the yellow prickly ash, has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant's roots, bark, and leaves are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties have been reported to have potential health benefits. In some African countries, the plant is used to treat conditions such as malaria and skin infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides.

side_effects: Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides, also known as the yellow sandalwood or African prickle-ash, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine. However, its use can cause various side effects due to its toxicity. The plant contains coumarin and other compounds that can be irritating to the skin and mucous membranes. Ingestion of the plant's parts can also cause gastrointestinal problems, such as nausea and vomiting. In addition, its seeds and leaves may cause allergic reactions in some individuals.
intro: Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides, commonly known as the yellow prickly ash, is a tropical plant species in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and distinctive, thorny branches. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Z. zanthoxyloides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as limonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Z. zanthoxyloides is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
